perturbation.original_text: (d) the Corporation has conducted its business in compliance, in all material respects, with all applicable laws, rules and regulations (including all applicable federal, national, provincial, municipal, and local environmental anti-pollution and licensing laws, regulations and other lawful requirements of any governmental or regulatory body, of each jurisdiction in which its business is carried on and is licensed, registered or qualified in all jurisdictions in which it owns, leases or operates its property or carries on business to enable its business to be carried on as now conducted and its property and assets to be owned, leased and operated and all such licences, registrations and qualifications are valid, subsisting and in good standing and it has not received a notice of non-compliance, nor knows of, nor has reasonable grounds to know of, any facts that could give rise to a notice of non-compliance with any such laws, regulations or permits which could reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect and all such licences, registrations and qualifications are valid, subsisting and in good standing;
perturbation.changed_text: (d) the Corporation has conducted its business in compliance with most applicable laws, rules and regulations. The Corporation's licences, registrations and qualifications are valid, subsisting and in good standing.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the Corporation's warranty regarding compliance with laws, specifically removing the 'in all material respects' qualifier regarding environmental and other crucial regulations. This increases the legal risk to investors by potentially hiding non-compliance issues.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification weakens the commitment to full compliance, which contradicts the spirit and requirements of NI 51-102 requiring continuous and timely disclosure of material information, including compliance with laws and regulations that could materially affect the company's operations or financial condition.
perturbation.justification: The change removes the 'in all material respects' qualifier and other specifics, weakening the original commitment to full compliance with environmental and other laws. This omission creates a legal loophole, potentially hiding non-compliance issues, as described in the perturbation explanation, and increasing legal risk. The Law Explanation points out that NI 51-102 requires timely disclosure of material information, including compliance with laws that materially affect the company, which this omission undermines by creating a potential for non-disclosure due to the weakened compliance warranty.

perturbation.original_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents, and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants, that: (a) the Corporation (i) is duly amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and is up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings and is in good standing under such Act; (ii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business as now conducted and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets (including as described in the Public Disclosure); and (iii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder;
perturbation.changed_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents that: (a) the Corporation (i) is amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and is in good standing under such Act; (ii) has corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business as now conducted and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets; and (iii) has corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder;
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase "and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants" weakens the reliance aspect, reducing the Agent's legal recourse if the representations are false.  Also, removing 'up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings' weakens the warranty.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Business Corporations Act (Ontario) places several requirements on corporations including timely and accurate filings. The removal of language that acknowledges the agents are relying on the representation increases the legal risk, and weakening the claim to be current with filings increases legal risks by not adhering to required filings.

Incorporation 3.1 Professional co
perturbation.justification: The original text includes an acknowledgment that the Agents are relying on the Corporation's representations, warranties, and covenants, and warrants that the corporation is up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings. The changed text omits 'and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants' and 'up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings and is'. The removal weakens the Agent's legal recourse if the representations are false and also weakens the warranty of compliance. The Law Explanation indicates the removal of language acknowledging reliance increases legal risk, and weakening the claim to be current with filings increases legal risks. Scraped Law Snippet 1 relates to the Business Corporations Act (Ontario), which, according to the Law Explanation, places requirements on corporations including timely and accurate filings. Thus, the omission contradicts the obligation to remain up-to-date with filings, creating a legal loophole that increases risk.

perturbation.original_text: (t) the execution and delivery of this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and the fulfilment of the terms hereof and thereof by the Corporation, including the issuance and sale of the Offered Shares, do not and will not (as the case may be) conflict with or result in a breach or violation of any of the terms or provisions of, or constitute a default under, whether after notice or lapse of time or both, (A) any statute, rule or regulation applicable to the Corporation, including Applicable Securities Laws; (B) the constating documents, by-laws or resolutions of the Corporation; (C) the terms of any Debt Instrument, Material Agreement, mortgage, note, indenture, instrument, lease or any other material agreement to which the Corporation is a party or by which they are bound; or (D) any judgment, decree or order binding the Corporation or the respective property or assets of the Corporation;
perturbation.changed_text: (t) the execution and delivery of this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and the fulfilment of the terms hereof and thereof by the Corporation, including the issuance and sale of the Offered Shares, do not and will not (as the case may be) conflict with or result in a breach or violation of any of the terms or provisions of, or constitute a default under, (B) the constating documents, by-laws or resolutions of the Corporation; (C) the terms of any Debt Instrument, Material Agreement, mortgage, note, indenture, instrument, lease or any other material agreement to which the Corporation is a party or by which they are bound; or (D) any judgment, decree or order binding the Corporation or the respective property or assets of the Corporation;
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the warranty that the agreement does not violate any statute, rule, or regulation, including Applicable Securities Laws. This is a significant omission, exposing the Agents to legal risk if the agreement does violate securities laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: By omitting the guarantee that the agreement does not violate any statute, rule, or regulation (including securities laws), the revised text potentially conflicts with the Ontario Securities Act, which regulates the issuance and sale of securities and aims to protect investors. If the agreement violates any provision of the Act, this omission makes the agreement misleading and increases legal risk.

CONTENTS Interpretation 1. Interpret
perturbation.justification: The omission of the warranty that the agreement does not violate any statute, rule, or regulation, including Applicable Securities Laws, directly contradicts the intent of securities regulations. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to the Securities Act, R.S.O. 1990, c. S.5, which is intended to regulate the issuance and sale of securities. By removing the guarantee of compliance with such laws, the modified contract exposes the agents to legal risk if the agreement violates these laws, thus undermining the protection intended by the statute and creating a legal contradiction under the definition of Omission Legal Contradiction.
